channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UK braces for environmental impact as ships burn in North Sea after collision
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British officials were bracing for environmental damage and seeking answers Tuesday after a cargo ship carrying a toxic chemical hit a tanker transporting jet fuel for the U.S. military off eastern England, setting both vessels ablaze.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Uganda deploys special forces to South Sudan to protect the government as fears of civil war grow
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Uganda has deployed an unknown number of troops to South Sudan in a bid to protect the fragile government of President Salva Kiir as a tense rivalry with his deputy threatens a return to civil war in the east African nation.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greenland votes in key election as Trump wants to take control of the strategic island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The single polling station in Greenland ’s capital city is ready. This big Arctic island with a tiny population holds early parliamentary elections Tuesday that are being closely watched. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope gets good news from doctors: An upgraded prognosis that he's no longer in immediate danger
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis woke up Tuesday to good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the double pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month in the longest and gravest threat to his 12-year papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's government signs a breakthrough deal with Kurdish-led authorities in the northeast
item.description: JABLEH, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim government signed a deal Monday with the Kurdish-led authority that controls the country’s northeast, including a ceasefire and the merging of the main U.S.-backed force there into the Syrian army.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cargo ship hits a tanker in fiery crash off England
item.description: A cargo ship struck a tanker Monday in a fiery crash in the North Sea off the coast of eastern England, causing fuel to stream into local waters. The tanker, named the MV Stena Immaculate, which carries oil and chemicals and features the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian war veteran amputees learn adaptive skiing in Oregon
item.description: HOODOO SKI AREA, Ore. (AP) — Ukrainian war veteran Oleksandr Shvachka lost his left leg to Russian tank fire outside Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and JENNY KANE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's next prime minister Carney says transition will be quick after meeting with Trudeau
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s next prime minister met with outgoing prime minister Justin Trudeau on Monday and vowed a quick transition. Mark Carney said he had a long meeting with Trudeau in which they discussed U.S.-Canada relations, national security issues and the timing of the handover in power.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctors declare Pope Francis no longer in imminent danger due to pneumonia, but remains hospitalized
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors said Monday Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a month, but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Court-martial convenes for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A court-martial convened on Monday for Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced in federal court last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents after the most consequential national security breach in years.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 30 nations will participate in Paris planning talks on a security force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Military officials from more than 30 nations will take part in Paris talks on the creation of an international security force for Ukraine, a French military official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: A cache of explosives in Sydney provoked antisemitic terrorism fears. Police now say it was faked
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — An explosives-filled trailer that Australian politicians earlier described as an antisemitic terrorism plot and a foiled mass casualty event was staged by criminals in a complicated hoax -- and was never meant to be detonated, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tokyo was filled with charred corpses after US firebombing 80 years ago. Survivors want compensation
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — More than 100,000 people were killed in a single night 80 years ago Monday in the U.S. firebombing of Tokyo, the Japanese capital. The attack, made with conventional bombs, destroyed downtown Tokyo and filled the streets with heaps of charred bodies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI and MAYUKO ONO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Widespread killing, rape, disease and war make Democratic Republic of Congo hell on earth
item.description: Fighting by Islamic State and other terrorist groups over minerals and power has led to widespread killings and rape in the DRC. Disease, child slavery and Christian persecution stalk the population.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of injured Hamas hostage directs plea to 'every mother in this world' to help secure son's release
item.description: Idit Ohel is fighting for the release of son Alon, who has been held hostage in Gaza since Oct. 7, 2023, and is at risk of going blind from an injury sustained in the Hamas attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis now eating solid foods, showing 'slight gradual improvements,' Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis is responding well to treatment and has shown a "gradual, mild improvement" in recent days as he continues his recovery from double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bipartisan legislation seeks to rein in Erdogan's Turkey over ties to US foes
item.description: A bipartisan group of congressmen have taken issue with NATO ally Turkey aligning its foreign policy interests against the U.S. and its Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN draft report on children in conflict zones raises eyebrows with framing of Israelis
item.description: The U.N.'s annual report on children in conflict zones, which is set to be published in June, is already getting attention for its framing of Israeli victims and for omitting Hamas crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization after giving world a sign of his frailty
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis hit the three-week mark Friday in his hospitalization for double pneumonia, in stable condition, receiving therapy but not necessarily improving, after giving the world a tangible indication of just how frail and sick he is.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and MARIA CHENG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump considering pressure on Russia and seeking deal with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said he’s “strongly considering” sanctions and tariffs on Russia in hopes of forcing a settlement to the war in Ukraine. Trump also sent a letter to Iran’s leaders seeking to negotiate a nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boy with loaded shotgun boarded plane in Australia, tackled by passenger
item.description: A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump envoy says Ukrainians 'brought it on themselves' after US pauses aid and intelligence sharing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukraine was given “fair warning” by the White House before President Donald Trump this week ordered a pause on U.S. military assistance and intelligence sharing with Kyiv, a senior administration official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tariffs not the way Trump should slow fentanyl flow: Bolton
item.description: "American importers pay the tariffs and then do everything they can to pass that cost on to American consumers," John Bolton said.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration pauses flow of intelligence to Ukraine that helps on battlefield
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe called the suspension a “pause” and said it came after the disastrous meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy in the Oval Office last week. Ratcliffe said Trump wanted to know that Zelenskyy was serious about peace.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese hackers, officials charged in cyberespionage campaign
item.description: The hacking by workers of I-Soon was done in some cases at the direction of China's Ministry of Public Security.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Israel’s cutoff of supplies to Gaza sends prices soaring as aid stockpiles dwindle
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s cutoff of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies to Gaza’s 2 million people has sent prices soaring and humanitarian groups into overdrive trying to distribute dwindling stocks to the most vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 12 killed, 30 wounded in Pakistan military base attack launched by suicide bombers
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two suicide bombings breached a wall at a military base in northwestern Pakistan before other attackers stormed the compound and were repelled in violence that killed at least 12 people and wounded 30 others, according to officials and a local hospital.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
